items.title: John Wall told me on Dybantsa: "He is like a young Tracy McGrady. Got the hesi-pull, super athletic, all the tools...Now you got a tall-ass lineup to help with Trae Young being a small guard. Kyshawn's 6-8, AJ's 6-9 -- might be one of the tallest lineups in the league" www.espn.c

media.title: Wizards opt for BYU's AJ Dybantsa with top pick of NBA draft
media.description: The Wizards ushered in a new era by drafting AJ Dybantsa with the first pick, selecting him over Kansas' Darryn Peterson to help turn the franchise around.

items.title: “You can see we had a type."
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
“You can see we had a type."

In Tuesday's NBA draft, the Spurs rolled the dice on an injury plagued center from Kentucky and spent draft capital to nab another bruising big man from Connecticut.

Brian Wright's draft-night manta was clear: Go big or go home.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

items.title: More from Brian Wright on Dylan Harper and De'Aaron Fox:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
More from Brian Wright on Dylan Harper and De'Aaron Fox:

"They're are a tough matchup. We'll continue to put them in situations for them to be at their best...They'll learn from this year on how they continue to play off one another and support one another and go from there."

items.title: Brian Wright said it will be up to Mitch Johnson to define Dylan Harper's role next season.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: Brian Wright said it will be up to Mitch Johnson to define Dylan Harper's role next season.

items.title: Asked Lawrence Frank about second round
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Lawrence Frank about second round

Mentioned that there are players who might be available to get drafted that teams would prefer on a 2-way, but reps will try and discourage teams from picking those players if there are other teams in draft that would prefer getting them on standard contract

items.title: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in De'Aaron (Fox) and who he is as a player, what he's been for us, and what he means to this team, and there's no wavering in that at all."
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in De'Aaron (Fox) and who he is as a player, what he's been for us, and what he means to this team, and there's no wavering in that at all."

items.author: Law Murray 📘

items.title: Wright said the Spurs have a "great group."
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Wright said the Spurs have a "great group."

"We've got a lot of that group coming back and so we'll continue to do our due diligence, but we believe in the team we had. We believe we were there (in the Finals) for a reason."

He also heaped praise on the Knicks saying they "earned" their title.

items.title: Brian Wright said he thought Mitch Johnson and his staff "did an amazing...phenomenal job."
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Brian Wright said he thought Mitch Johnson and his staff "did an amazing...phenomenal job."

He added that everyone in the organization can find moments in the season "where we can all get better, and that's a beautiful thing."

items.title: Today I stepped off a flight from Europe and barely had time to get my thoughts together before heading over to the Jazz facility for draft night. I barely know what my name is, much less what day it is. I just hope what I wrote was moderately coherent.
items.author: Sarah Todd
items.body_excerpt: Today I stepped off a flight from Europe and barely had time to get my thoughts together before heading over to the Jazz facility for draft night. I barely know what my name is, much less what day it is. I just hope what I wrote was moderately coherent.

items.title: On Saturday, the Jazz brass went to Darryn Peterson’s hometown of Canton, Ohio. They spent time with him and his family, sold him on the Jazz and solidified the decision that he was their guy.
items.author: Sarah Todd
items.body_excerpt: On Saturday, the Jazz brass went to Darryn Peterson’s hometown of Canton, Ohio. They spent time with him and his family, sold him on the Jazz and solidified the decision that he was their guy.

media.title: A special trip to Ohio by Jazz decision makers sealed the deal on selecting Darryn Peterson
media.description: The Utah Jazz selected Darryn Peterson with the second overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

items.title: Brian Wright on the Spurs' improbable run to the Finals:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Brian Wright on the Spurs' improbable run to the Finals:

"It was obviously a great season and a lot to learn and grow from. And I know the guys will take that in the summer and continue to improve."

items.title: Soooo, Kings fans....How are we feelin?

We just dropped a brand new episode on night one of the draft

items.title: Tarris Reed Jr. loved the "physicality" he saw in the Finals.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Tarris Reed Jr. loved the "physicality" he saw in the Finals.

"Guys were in the paint, hacking, bruising, all that. I got the body for it and I embrace the physical game.That's a huge part of who I am and how I play."

items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Some scattered 2026 NBA Draft notes:

🏀Only four NCAA Early Entry Candidates did not get drafted in the first round: I. Evans, M. Thomas, B. Tucker, H. Veesaar
🏀Six seniors were drafted in the first-round: Y. Lendeborg, B. Stirtz, Z. Ejiofor, T. Reed Jr, J. Jefferson, A. Karaban

1/2

items.title: Going through Trajan's quotes, one thing that sticks out is how much he emphasizes Okorie's ability to generate paint touches in halfcourt. The offense was very transition-reliant last year, which hurt them in the playoffs. Okorie addresses that.

items.body_excerpt: Going through Trajan's quotes, one thing that sticks out is how much he emphasizes Okorie's ability to generate paint touches in halfcourt. The offense was very transition-reliant last year, which hurt them in the playoffs. Okorie addresses that.

Brian Wright said Spurs are comfortable w/ possibly taking a very long-term approach" w/ Quaintance. 

"He's got tremendous, tremendous talent and unfortunately that was cut short a little bit due to injury. But had it not been, maybe we don't have the opportunity to draft him."

items.title: Some tax/apron-related fallout from tonight's draft pick trades:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Some tax/apron-related fallout from tonight's draft pick trades:

🏀CLE: Shaved $2.9M off by trading away #29. Now $21.1M under the tax, $29.1M under first apron and $42.1M under second apron (before re-signing James Harden - assuming he opts out)

1/3

items.title: Hell of a Yaxel Lendeborg stat from the Warriors: In 2024-25, Lendeborg's last season at UAB before transferring to Michigan, he became the second player ever to have 600 points, 400 rebounds and 150 assists in a season

items.body_excerpt:
Hell of a Yaxel Lendeborg stat from the Warriors: In 2024-25, Lendeborg's last season at UAB before transferring to Michigan, he became the second player ever to have 600 points, 400 rebounds and 150 assists in a season 

Larry Bird was the other player to do so

He later pointed talked about Shannon and Beringer as internal development guys
